1	\chapter{The relative cotangent presheaf}
2	\label{chap:differentials}
3	
4	This chapter develops the relative cotangent \emph{presheaf} $\Omega_{X/S}$ of a morphism of schemes $f \colon X \to S$, starting from the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ already available in Mathlib. The output is a presheaf of $\struct{X}$-modules whose sections on each open $V \subseteq X$ are the K\"ahler differential module of the ring map $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) \to \struct{X}(V)$ induced by $f$, where $f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}$ denotes the \emph{presheaf-level} inverse-image functor (left Kan extension along $(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,f.\mathrm{base}).\mathrm{op}$).
5	
6	The downstream role of this chapter is to provide the \emph{forward direction} of the standard smoothness criterion: smoothness of $f$ implies that on every affine chart $V \to U$ (with $V \subseteq X$, $U \subseteq S$ affine and $f V \subseteq U$) the appLE-algebra K\"ahler differential $\Omega_{\Gamma(X, V)\,/\,\Gamma(S, U)}$ is a free $\Gamma(X, V)$-module of rank $n$. This is the Jacobian-criterion direction and is what connects to the dimension hypothesis on the Jacobian: the smoothness of the curve $C \to \Spec k$ in the Jacobian definition is the special case $n = 1$, and the relative dimension of $\Jac(C)$ is read off from the rank via the same forward implication. The bridge between this affine-chart-wise algebra-K\"ahler form and the section module of the relative cotangent presheaf $\Omega_{X/S}$ was the former content of milestone~M1; the bridge declaration was excised iter-126 (zero in-tree consumers) and replaced in this chapter by the standalone K\"ahler-localization utilities of Section~\ref{sec:bridge}, the off-loop Mathlib-PR work of which proceeds from \texttt{analogies/relative-differentials-presheaf-bridge.md}. The converse implication of the forward smoothness criterion is \emph{mathematically false} as stated and is documented separately in Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}.
7	
8	\section{The relative cotangent presheaf}
9	
10	\begin{definition}
11	
12	\leanok
13	[Relative cotangent presheaf of a morphism]
14	  \label{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
15	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}
16	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes. The presheaf-level inverse-image presheaf of rings $f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S}$ on $X$ (the left Kan extension of $\struct{S}$ along $(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,f.\mathrm{base}).\mathrm{op}$, given on each open $V \subseteq X$ by the colimit $\mathrm{colim}_{W : \,f V \subseteq W \subseteq S}\,\struct{S}(W)$) is paired with $\struct{X}$ by the canonical adjunction-transpose $f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S} \to \struct{X}$ of the comorphism $f^{\sharp}\colon\struct{S}\to f_{*}\struct{X}$. Applying the presheaf-of-modules K\"ahler differential construction $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}'$ to this map of presheaves of commutative rings produces a presheaf of $\struct{X}$-modules on $X$, the \emph{relative cotangent presheaf} $\Omega_{X/S}$.
17	\end{definition}
18	
19	\begin{lemma}
20	
21	\leanok
22	[Sections of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on an open]
23	  \label{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
24	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler}
25	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
26	  For every open $V \subseteq X$, the sections of the relative cotangent presheaf are, by construction, the K\"ahler differential module of the ring map $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) \to \struct{X}(V)$ induced by $f$:
27	  \[
28	    \Omega_{X/S}(V) \;=\; \Omega_{\struct{X}(V) \,/\, (f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V)}.
29	  \]
30	  Here $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) = \mathrm{colim}_{W : \,f V \subseteq W \subseteq S}\,\struct{S}(W)$ is the presheaf-level inverse-image section ring, \emph{not} $\struct{S}(U)$ for an arbitrary affine open $U \supseteq f V$. The relationship between this colimit-source K\"ahler module and the classical ring-theoretic $\Omega_{B/A}$ for $A = \struct{S}(U)$, $B = \struct{X}(V)$ on an affine chart $V = \Spec B \subseteq f^{-1}U$ with $U = \Spec A \subseteq S$ affine is the content of the bridge documented in Section~\ref{sec:bridge}.
31	\end{lemma}
32	
33	\begin{proof}
34	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
35	  \leanok
36	  By definition of $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}'$: the construction is built object-wise to be the Mathlib \texttt{KaehlerDifferential} module of the ring map at each open. The identification is therefore by \texttt{rfl} after unfolding the definition. The colimit description of $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V)$ is the definition of \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback} as the left Kan extension along $(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,f.\mathrm{base}).\mathrm{op}$ in Mathlib.
37	\end{proof}
38	
39	\begin{remark}[Comparison with Mathlib's ring-level $\Omega_{B/A}$]
40	  \label{rem:kahler_compatibility}
41	  Mathlib provides the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ together with the universal derivation $\mathrm{d} \colon B \to \Omega_{B/A}$. The construction of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on each open is, by definition, this Mathlib module applied at the ring level of the presheaf, so all ring-level Mathlib lemmas about $\Omega_{B/A}$ transfer verbatim under Lemma~\ref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj} — but for the \emph{colimit-source} ring $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V)$, not for $\struct{S}(U)$. Transferring to the classical $A = \struct{S}(U)$ source on an affine chart is the bridge of Section~\ref{sec:bridge}.
42	\end{remark}
43	
44	\section{Smoothness criterion for $\Omega_{X/S}$ (forward direction)}
45	
46	The forward direction of the textbook smoothness criterion identifies a necessary condition on the relative K\"ahler differentials: every point of $X$ admits an affine chart $V \to U$ over which the appLE-algebra K\"ahler module $\Omega_{B/A}$ is free of the prescribed rank, with $A = \struct{S}(U)$ and $B = \struct{X}(V)$. This is the global version of the algebraic-side fact that an \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~algebra has a free K\"ahler differential module of rank $n$. We state and prove the theorem directly on the appLE-algebra K\"ahler module, sidestepping the presheaf bridge of Section~\ref{sec:bridge}. The converse implication (a local-freeness hypothesis on $\Omega$ alone implying smoothness) is \emph{false}; see Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}.
47	
48	\begin{theorem}\leanok
49	[Smooth implies $\Omega$ locally free (algebra-K\"ahler form)]
50	  \label{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}
51	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega}
52	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
53	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes and let $n \in \mathbb{N}$. If $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$, then for every point $x \in X$ there exist an affine open $U \subseteq S$, an affine open $V \subseteq X$ with $x \in V$ and $V \subseteq f^{-1}(U)$, such that the K\"ahler differential module
54	  \[
55	    \Omega_{\Gamma(X, V) \,/\, \Gamma(S, U)}
56	  \]
57	  (over the appLE algebra structure $\Gamma(S, U) \to \Gamma(X, V)$ induced by $f$) is a free $\Gamma(X, V)$-module of rank $n$.
58	\end{theorem}
59	
60	\begin{proof}
61	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
62	  \leanok
63	  Fix $x \in X$. We use the abbreviation $A := \Gamma(S, U)$ and $B := \Gamma(X, V)$ for the section rings on the affine chart we will produce; the algebra map $A \to B$ is the appLE map $(f.\mathrm{appLE}\,U\,V\,e).\mathrm{hom}$ induced by $f$.
64	
65	  \emph{Step 1: extract a standard-smooth chart at $x$.} The auto-generated \texttt{mk\_iff} bridge
66	  \[
67	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.smoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} \quad [\text{verified}]
68	  \]
69	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth}; produced by the \texttt{@[mk\_iff]} attribute on the class \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}) unfolds the hypothesis \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$f$ into: for every $x \in X$, there exist affine opens $U \subseteq S$ and $V \subseteq X$ with $x \in V$ and $V \leq f^{-1}U$, such that the induced algebra map $(f.\mathrm{appLE}\,U\,V\,e).\mathrm{hom}\colon A \to B$ satisfies \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$A$~$B$.
70	
71	  \emph{Step 2: pass to plain standard-smooth.} The lemma
72	  \[
73	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth} \quad [\text{verified}]
74	  \]
75	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmooth}) yields \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth}~$A$~$B$ from the relative-dimension-tagged form, unlocking the cotangent-module machinery in the next two steps.
76	
77	  \emph{Step 3: free K\"ahler module on the chart.} The Mathlib instance
78	  \[
79	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
80	  \]
81	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}) synthesises \texttt{Module.Free}~$B$~$\Omega_{B/A}$ from \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth}~$A$~$B$.
82	
83	  \emph{Step 4: pin the rank.} The theorem
84	  \[
85	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
86	  \]
87	  (same file as Step 3) gives $\rank_B\,\Omega_{B/A} = n$ under the hypotheses \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$ and \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$A$~$B$.
88	
89	  \emph{Step 4.5: discharge the \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$ side condition.} The chart witness $x \in V$ shows that the open $V \subseteq X$ is non-empty as a set, so its structure-sheaf section ring $B = \Gamma(X, V) = \struct{X}(V)$ is non-zero by \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.component\_nontrivial} (the structure sheaf of a scheme has \texttt{Nontrivial} sections on a non-empty open). This supplies the \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$ hypothesis required by Step 4.
90	
91	  Combining Steps 1--4.5, the chart $(U, V, e)$ produced by Step 1 satisfies $x \in V$, $\mathrm{IsAffineOpen}\,U$, $\mathrm{IsAffineOpen}\,V$, and under the appLE algebra structure $A = \Gamma(S, U) \to B = \Gamma(X, V)$, the K\"ahler module $\Omega_{B/A}$ is a free $B$-module of rank $n$. As $x \in X$ was arbitrary, the statement follows.
92	
93	  This is the forward direction of the Jacobian criterion: Stacks Tag~02G1 (and Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2 in the Noetherian case).
94	
95	  \emph{Mathlib name summary.} The five \texttt{[verified]} closure pieces the proof relies on are
96	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.smoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} (Step 1),
97	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth} (Step 2),
98	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} (Step 3),
99	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} (Step 4),
100	  and \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.component\_nontrivial} (Step 4.5).
101	\end{proof}
102	
103	\begin{remark}[Lean naming]
104	  \label{rem:smooth_class_naming}
105	  The Lean class for ``smooth of relative dimension $n$'' is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.SmoothOfRelativeDimension}; the spelling \texttt{IsSmoothOfRelativeDimension} that appeared in earlier iterations of this project is a deprecated alias in Mathlib \texttt{b80f227}. The \texttt{\textbackslash lean\{...\}} hint above names the non-deprecated form.
106	\end{remark}
107	
108	\section{Standalone K\"ahler-localization utilities (post-iter-126 M1 excise)}
109	\label{sec:bridge}
110	
111	The presheaf-form $\Omega_{X/S}$ of Definition~\ref{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf} and the algebra-K\"ahler form $\Omega_{B / A}$ on an affine chart of Theorem~\ref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} are connected, on an affine chart, by a canonical $B$-linear isomorphism. Earlier iterations of the project (iter-121 through iter-125) hosted this isomorphism in the Lean tree as a \emph{bridge} declaration \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_equiv\_kaehler\_appLE} backed by a Mathlib gap-fill sub-lemma \texttt{appLE\_isLocalization} (the load-bearing localization-at-the-image-submonoid claim, packaged as a multi-LOC residual sorry whose closure was concrete but had no in-tree consumer). The forward-direction smoothness criterion (Theorem~\ref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}) closed iter-120 in algebra-K\"ahler form \emph{independently} of the bridge, so the bridge was project-local infrastructure with zero downstream consumer.
112	
113	\textbf{Excise decision (iter-126).} Per the iter-126 strategy-critic CHALLENGE, ratified by the iter-126 user hint endorsing "shortest path qualified by your role is to fill mathlib gaps", the bridge declaration and its load-bearing sub-lemma were excised from the Lean tree iter-126: closing the bridge produced neither a downstream consumer nor a Mathlib PR (the extractable PR candidate is M1.d — Lemma~\ref{lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso} below — and is \emph{independent} of the bridge). The two retained declarations in this section are the standalone K\"ahler-localization utilities that survive as upstream-PR-quality material; off-loop PR work proceeds from \texttt{analogies/relative-differentials-presheaf-bridge.md}.
114	
115	\begin{lemma}\leanok
116	[K\"ahler module of a localization is trivial]
117	  \label{lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton}
118	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.kaehler_localization_subsingleton}
119	  Let $A \to L$ be a localization at a multiplicative subset $M \subseteq A$. Then $\Omega_{L / A}$ is subsingleton (the zero $L$-module).
120	\end{lemma}
121	
122	\begin{proof}
123	
124	\leanok
125	  This is a thin re-export of Mathlib's existing infrastructure: \texttt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of\_isLocalization}~\texttt{[verified]} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Unramified.Basic}, line 303) produces the instance \texttt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified}~$A$~$L$ from any \texttt{IsLocalization}~$M$~$L$ predicate; the instance \texttt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified.subsingleton\_kaehlerDifferential}~\texttt{[verified]} (same file, lines 57--59) then yields \texttt{Subsingleton}~$(\mathrm{KaehlerDifferential}~A~L)$. Hence the project-side lemma is a two-line re-export ($\mathtt{haveI} := \mathtt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of\_isLocalization}~M$ \texttt{; infer\_instance}); contra an earlier framing, no Mathlib gap-fill is required for this lemma.
126	\end{proof}
127	
128	\begin{lemma}\leanok
129	[Tower-cancellation equivalence for K\"ahler modules under localization]
130	  \label{lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso}
131	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.kaehler_quotient_localization_iso}
132	  \uses{lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton}
133	  Let $A \to L$ be a localization (in the sense of \texttt{IsLocalization}), and let $L \to B$ be a ring map forming a scalar tower $A \to L \to B$. Then the canonical $B$-linear map $\Omega_{B/A} \to \Omega_{B/L}$ induced by the tower is a $B$-linear equivalence.
134	\end{lemma}
135	
136	\begin{proof}
137	  \uses{lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton}
138	  \leanok
139	  The second fundamental exact sequence of K\"ahler differentials reads
140	  \[
141	    B \otimes_{L} \Omega_{L/A} \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{B/A} \;\twoheadrightarrow\; \Omega_{B/L} \;\longrightarrow\; 0.
142	  \]
143	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton}, the left term vanishes (since $\Omega_{L/A}$ is subsingleton and tensoring against it gives the zero module). Therefore the right surjection has zero kernel and is a $B$-linear equivalence. The Mathlib closure pieces are \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.exact\_mapBaseChange\_map}~\texttt{[verified]} (the exact sequence above) and \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.map\_surjective}~\texttt{[verified]} (the right-hand surjection), both in \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic}; the project-side declaration packages these into a \texttt{LinearEquiv} via \texttt{LinearEquiv.ofBijective}. This is the most extractable Mathlib contribution candidate of milestone~M1: a clean generalisation of \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquivOfFormallyEtale} to the ``only the lower step is formally unramified'' case, candidate name \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.equivOfFormallyUnramified}.
144	\end{proof}

151	\section{Converse direction (milestone M4)}
152	\label{sec:converse-out-of-scope}
153	
154	This direction is recorded as an optional future milestone~M4 (see \texttt{STRATEGY.md}). Closure requires the deformation-theoretic hypothesis \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)}, which the project may wire up if a downstream consumer demands the iff form. The remainder of this section documents the obstruction in concrete form: a counterexample showing that without that hypothesis the converse fails, and a comparison with the Mathlib converse lemma at the ring level.
155	
156	The reverse implication
157	\[
158	  \forall x\in X,\ \exists\,U\text{ affine},\ \Omega_{X/S}(U)\text{ free of rank }n
159	  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad f \text{ smooth of relative dimension } n
160	\]
161	is \emph{mathematically false} as stated, even under the auxiliary hypothesis that $f$ is locally of finite presentation. Local freeness of $\Omega$ alone does not imply flatness, and flatness (equivalently, formal smoothness via vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology) is genuine deformation-theoretic content not encoded in the section-module hypothesis. This is why the iff form of the criterion present in earlier iterations was demoted to a forward implication.
162	
163	\begin{remark}[Explicit counterexample]
164	  \label{rem:converse_counterexample}
165	  Let $k$ be a field and consider the morphism $f \colon \Spec k \to \Spec k[t]$ induced by the surjection $k[t] \twoheadrightarrow k$ sending $t \mapsto 0$. Then $f$ is locally of finite presentation, its source $\Spec k$ has a unique affine open (itself), and the K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{k/k[t]} = 0$ is free of rank $0$ (the trivial \texttt{Module.Basis} indexed by the empty set witnesses this). Yet $f$ is the closed immersion of a non-open point of $\Spec k[t]$, hence is not flat and therefore not smooth, so \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$0$~$f$ fails. The local-freeness hypothesis on $\Omega$ is therefore strictly weaker than smoothness, and the converse implication of the iff form fails on this very example.
166	\end{remark}
167	
168	\begin{remark}[The Mathlib converse lemma and its hypotheses]
169	  \label{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}
170	  Mathlib does provide a converse-direction lemma at the ring level,
171	  \[
172	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.of\_basis\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
173	  \]
174	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothOfFree}). Its three hypotheses make the missing content explicit:
175	  \begin{itemize}
176	    \item \texttt{Algebra.FinitePresentation}~$A$~$B$;
177	    \item \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} --- vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology, i.e.\ formal smoothness;
178	    \item a basis $b \colon \texttt{Module.Basis}~I~B~\Omega_{B/A}$ whose range lies in the image of the universal derivation, $\mathrm{Set.range}\,(\texttt{Algebra.KaehlerDifferential.D}~A~B)$.
179	  \end{itemize}
180	  None of these three is supplied by the section-module hypothesis of the would-be iff. The first-cotangent-vanishing hypothesis in particular is the deformation-theoretic input that distinguishes ``$\Omega$ locally free'' from ``smooth''; without it, the counterexample of Remark~\ref{rem:converse_counterexample} would already break the converse. Establishing \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} from project-side hypotheses (e.g.\ via a flatness assumption added to the input) is the work that would close the converse, and is the content of milestone~M4.
181	\end{remark}
182	
183	\begin{remark}[Cross-reference to Stacks 02G1]
184	  \label{rem:stacks_02G1}
185	  The standard textbook biconditional ``$f$ is smooth of relative dimension $d$ iff $f$ is flat, locally of finite presentation, and $\Omega_{X/S}$ is locally free of rank $d$'' is Stacks Tag~02G1 (and is the Noetherian-case Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2). The flatness conjunct is precisely what is missing from a hypothesis that only asserts local freeness of $\Omega$; supplying it amounts to the deformation-theoretic content discussed in Remark~\ref{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}. The forward implication proved as Theorem~\ref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} above corresponds to the easy direction of the same tag.
186	\end{remark}
187	
188	\section{Content scheduled for later milestones}
189	
190	Each bullet below is a future milestone whose Mathlib infrastructure is not yet available in the pinned snapshot \texttt{b80f227}; inclusion in this list does \emph{not} mean the project has abandoned it. The roadmap progression is: M1 (this iter) $\to$ M2 / M3 (existence routes) $\to$ M4 (converse, Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}) $\to$ M5+ (sheafification, cotangent exact sequence, etc., listed here). Each item lists the deferred concept, the missing Mathlib piece, and one cross-reference for forward-looking reinstatement.
191	
192	\begin{itemize}
193	  \item \textbf{Sheaf condition for $\Omega_{X/S}$ (M5).} The relative cotangent presheaf is, on an affine basis, locally isomorphic to Mathlib's quasi-coherent sheaf $\widetilde{\Omega_{B/A}}$ on each affine chart, and the unique-gluing characterisation of the sheaf property holds basis-wise. No off-the-shelf Mathlib lemma packages ``\texttt{Scheme.PresheafOfModules}-sheaf-on-affine-basis $\Rightarrow$ sheaf on $X$'' however; the cofinality descent against \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf\_iff\_isSheafOpensLeCover} is the deferred work for this milestone. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.5 (sheaves from affine charts) and Stacks Tag~009H.
194	
195	  \item \textbf{Cotangent exact sequence (M6).} For a composition of morphisms $X \to Y \to S$, there is an exact sequence of quasi-coherent $\struct{X}$-modules
196	  \[
197	    f^{*}\Omega_{Y/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\alpha\;}\; \Omega_{X/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\beta\;}\;
198	      \Omega_{X/Y} \;\longrightarrow\; 0.
199	  \]
200	  The ring-level ingredients (\texttt{KaehlerDifferential.exact\_mapBaseChange\_map} and the surjectivity of the right map) are in Mathlib, but the exactness conjunct requires \texttt{SheafOfModules.exact\_iff\_stalkwise} or an equivalent section-wise+sheafification route through homology-preserving instances of the forget functor; both are Mathlib gaps. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Proposition~8.4A and Stacks Tag~01UV.
201	
202	  \item \textbf{Cotangent space at a section (M7).} A corollary of the smoothness criterion above, pulled back along a section $s \colon S \to X$: if $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$ and $s$ is a section, then $s^{*}\Omega_{X/S}$ is a locally free $\struct{S}$-module of rank $n$. The natural Lean framing uses \texttt{Scheme.Modules.pullback} against the sheaf-bundled $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}$, which was trimmed in iter-117; a presheaf-form analogue against \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback} would need a separate formulation. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Corollary~8.10.
203	
204	  \item \textbf{Serre-duality genus identity (M8).} On a smooth proper geometrically integral curve $C/k$,
205	  \[
206	    \dim_{k} H^{0}(C, \Omega_{C/k}) \;=\; \dim_{k} H^{1}(C, \struct{C}),
207	  \]
208	  both sides being equal to the genus $g(C)$. Mathlib \texttt{b80f227} has no dualizing sheaf, no trace morphism for proper morphisms, and no Zariski coherent cohomology of $\struct{X}$-modules, so the equality cannot yet be expressed within the project's Mathlib snapshot. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~III.7 and Stacks Tag~0FVU.
209	\end{itemize}

3	## Goal
4	
5	Formalize Christian Merten's Jacobian challenge (`references/challenge.lean`):
6	nine protected declarations, headlined by `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian` and
7	`Jacobian.nonempty_jacobianWitness` — the existence of an Albanese / Jacobian
8	object uniform over the $k$-rational pointing of a smooth proper geometrically
9	irreducible curve `C/k`, with **no** `C(k) ≠ ∅` hypothesis on the protected
10	signature. End-state: zero inline `sorry`, kernel-only axioms.
11	
12	The protected signature quantifies over arbitrary `C` with no $C(k) \neq \emptyset$
13	assumption. The witness OBJECT `J` is always real (constructed unconditionally);
14	only `isAlbaneseFor` is universally quantified over `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`. The spine is
15	**pointed vs. unpointed**, not genus-0 vs. positive. `genus C := dim_k H^1(C,O_C)`
16	(arithmetic genus; protected — cannot be re-typed).

32	## Routes
33	
34	The positive-genus OBJECT is built by **Route A (Picard scheme via FGA)** —
35	mandatory and essentially unavoidable. The genus-0 arm is a separate, lower-risk
36	rigidity statement (object trivial); its base-case proof route is **Route C (Milne
37	§I.3 rigidity completion)**, chosen because Milne §I.3 + §III.6 derive both the
38	genus-0 base case AND Route A's Albanese UP cube-free, and a Mathlib-support survey
39	found Route C far less blocked than the differential route.

41	### Route A (COMMITTED, parallel-decomposed iter-171) — Picard scheme / Albanese via FGA
42

44	property. Decomposition A.1–A.4 in `Jacobian.tex` § "Route A". Literature in-tree:
45	`references/kleiman-picard.pdf` (§4 existence, §5 `Pic⁰`), `references/nitsure-hilbert-quot.pdf`
46	(Quot/Hilbert engine). **Positive-genus arm:** `J := Pic⁰_{C/k}`, a dim-`g`
47	abelian variety; the Albanese UP gives `isAlbaneseFor`. Critical path: the object
48	must be a real dim-`g` abelian variety even when `C(k)=∅`, so the FGA engine is
49	required unconditionally.
50	
51	**iter-171 parallel commitment.** A.1, A.3, A.4 are each blueprint-decomposed
52	this iter into prover-ready sub-chapters (~500 LOC sub-blocks), opening
53	parallel lanes from iter-172 onward (rather than the previous "serialise behind
54	genus-0 stack" stance which the strategy-critic iter-170 challenged). A.2's

59	### Route C (COMMITTED) — genus-0 rigidity completion via Milne §I.3
60	
61	The genus-0 witness OBJECT is the trivial `J = Spec k` (skeleton 6/7 closed); it
62	needs **no** Picard scheme. Remaining content: over `k̄`, every pointed `f : C → A`
63	from a genus-0 curve to an abelian variety is constant. The **Rigidity Lemma**
64	(Mumford Form I = Milne Rigidity Thm 1.1) is PROVEN axiom-clean and is the
65	foundation of this route, together with the proven **Cor 1.5** (additivity) and
66	**Cor 1.2** (AV maps are homs). The base case `ℙ¹→A const` is reached by the **`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut** — NO theorem of
67	the cube, NO Thm 3.2/Lemma 3.3, NO Auslander–Buchsbaum, NO `Hom(𝔾_a,A)=0`, NO
68	differentials/Frobenius; char-general; uses only the already-proven Cor 1.5 + density.
69	Chain:
70	
71	- **`σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m → ℙ¹`** the `𝔾_m`-scaling action `(x,λ)↦λx`: a TOTAL scheme morphism
72	  fixing the point `0`. The only genuinely new ingredient. Needs concrete ℙ¹ and 𝔾_m as
73	  group objects over `Spec k̄`.
74	- **`h := σ_× ≫ f`**; normalise `f(0)=η`. Apply `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`
75	  (**Cor 1.5**, first-factor-only-proper) with `V=ℙ¹` (proper), `W=𝔾_m`, base points
76	  `0,1`: the W-axis restriction `λ↦f(λ·0)=f(0)=η` COLLAPSES (0 is a scaling fixed point),
77	  so the decomposition degenerates to `σ_× ≫ f = pr₁ ≫ f`, i.e. `f(λx)=f(x)`.
78	- At `x=1`: `f|_{𝔾_m}≡f(1)` constant; **density** of `𝔾_m` in `ℙ¹` + `A` separated
79	  (`ext_of_isDominant`, present, used by `rigidity_core`) ⟹ `f` constant.
80	- **genus-0⟹ℙ¹** (RR bridge, Hartshorne IV.1.3.5) — shared, no Mathlib RR.
81	
82	Why no Thm 3.2 / no `Hom(𝔾_a,A)=0`: the apparent Thm-3.2 circularity was illusory (it
83	conflated the base case with Milne's *general* Thm 3.4 for a NON-complete source); here
84	`f` is already total on the complete `ℙ¹`. The `𝔾_a`-additive route (`f(x+y)=f(x)+f(y)`
85	then `Hom(𝔾_a,A)=0`) was a stepping-stone but `Hom(𝔾_a,A)=0` standalone needs "image of a
86	group hom is a closed subgroup" (a Mathlib gap as deep as Thm 3.2); the `𝔾_m`-scaling
87	shortcut sidesteps it by exploiting the scaling fixed point so Cor 1.5 degenerates
88	directly. The full Thm 3.2 / codim-1 extension is now a **Route-A-only** item (Albanese
89	UP), off the genus-0 path. Survey: `analogies/route-support.md`, `analogies/thm32-extend.md`.

94	  `dim Pic⁰=0` byproduct of Route A**, **(a) Serre-duality `df=0`** (artifact:
95	  `rigidity_over_kbar` `[CharZero]` in `RigidityKbar.lean`). All rejected/superseded
96	  in favour of the direct `ℙ¹×𝔾_a` argument.

1	# Analogy: Mathlib backing for `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "RR bridge")
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	rrbridge
8	
9	## Iteration
10	168
11	
12	## Question
13	
14	The progress-critic iter-168 raised a dispatch CHALLENGE: is
15	`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (`AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:1135`)
16	dispatchable as a parallel Lane B prover lane this iter, or does it require
17	upstream chapter expansion / multi-iter Mathlib sub-build first? In particular:
18	
19	- (Q1) Does Mathlib have a Riemann–Roch for curves?
20	- (Q2) Does Mathlib have Weil/Cartier divisors, a degree map `Pic → ℤ`,
21	  a `Mor(C, ℙ¹)` classification, or Hurwitz/Castelnuovo at scheme level?
22	- (Q3) Of the four Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 ingredients — (i) divisor of a closed
23	  point, (ii) RR dimension formula, (iii) linear equivalence, (iv) "rational
24	  ⟹ ≅ ℙ¹" — which are in Mathlib at scheme level?
25	- (Q4) If not dispatchable, what upstream chapter / Mathlib infrastructure
26	  must the planner schedule?
27	- (Q5) Is `Nonempty (C ≅ ProjectiveLineBar kbar)` the right Lean shape?
28	
29	## Project artifact(s)
30	
31	- `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:1135` — current statement
32	  `genusZero_curve_iso_P1 : … (_hgenus : genus C = 0) : Nonempty (C ≅ ProjectiveLineBar kbar)`
33	  with `sorry` body.
34	- `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:1160` — the only consumer
35	  `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme`, which `obtain ⟨φ⟩ := genusZero_curve_iso_P1 _hgenus`
36	  and transports `f` along `φ`.
37	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean:40` — project's `genus C := Module.finrank k
38	  (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)` (project's own `ModuleCat k`-flavoured
39	  `H¹(C, O_C)`).
40	- `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex:1448–1501` — the
41	  `prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1` block plus `rmk:genusZero_iso_subbuild`
42	  ("Mathlib has no Riemann–Roch theorem for curves, no divisor class group
43	  machinery at this level, and no ℙ¹-classification of rational curves").
44	
45	## Decisions identified
46	
47	### Decision 1: Is there a Mathlib Riemann–Roch theorem to invoke?
48	
49	- **Mathlib idiom**: There is *no* Riemann–Roch theorem for algebraic curves
50	  at scheme level. LSP probes:
51	  - `lean_leansearch "Riemann-Roch theorem for curves"` returns only
52	    Weierstrass-curve polynomial-degree results
53	    (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.EllipticCurve.*`), nothing geometric.
54	  - `lean_loogle "RiemannRoch"` returns **no hits**.
55	  - `lean_leansearch "scheme genus zero rational curve P1"` returns only
56	    `WeierstrassCurve.Projective.*` (elliptic-curve point arithmetic).
57	  - `lean_leansearch "smooth proper curve dimension cohomology"` returns
58	    `IsSmoothOfRelativeDimension`, `H1Cotangent` — definitions, no theorems.
59	  - The only "RR-shaped" data in Mathlib is in
60	    `Mathlib.Analysis.Meromorphic.Divisor` (`MeromorphicOn.divisor` —
61	    analytic, normed-field-valued, not algebraic), and inside
62	    `WeierstrassCurve.*` (division polynomials of *elliptic* curves —
63	    not curves of genus 0).
64	- **Project's path**: `prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1` proof block cites
65	  Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 directly (RR on `D = P − Q`, then Hartshorne II.6.10.1
66	  to upgrade `P ∼ Q` to a degree-1 map to ℙ¹). The Lean body is
67	  `sorry`-stubbed pending a sub-build.
68	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost — but the divergence is *upstream*, not a
69	  project misdesign. Mathlib has no RR; there is no idiom to align with.
70	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL.
71	
72	### Decision 2: Is divisor / Pic / degree machinery available?
73	
74	- **Mathlib idiom**: All four required pieces are *absent* at scheme level:
75	  - **Weil/Cartier divisors on a scheme**: ABSENT. `lean_loogle "Divisor"`
76	    returns no hits. The only `divisor` is `MeromorphicOn.divisor`
77	    (`Mathlib.Analysis.Meromorphic.Divisor`), which is the order function of
78	    a meromorphic function on a subset of a normed field — *not* a
79	    formal-sum-of-codim-1-cycles on a scheme.
80	  - **Picard group of a scheme**: ABSENT. `CommRing.Pic R`
81	    (`Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`) is *ring-level only* (invertible
82	    `R`-modules under tensor product). There is no `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic`
83	    in Mathlib (the project once had a `Scheme.Pic` scaffold in
84	    `.archon/lanes/{kimi,anthropic,deepseek}/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundle.lean`
85	    but those snapshots are *project-internal experimental code*, not
86	    Mathlib — they were never landed).
87	  - **Degree map `Pic → ℤ` for a curve**: ABSENT (precondition `Scheme.Pic`
88	    missing).
89	  - **`Mor(C, ℙ¹)` ≅ "degree-1 line bundle + 2 sections" classification**:
90	    ABSENT. Mathlib's closest tool is `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`
91	    (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic`), which packages
92	    "two compatible degree-1 global sections of the graded ring give a
93	    morphism into `Proj 𝒜`". The project ALREADY uses this in
94	    `Genus0BaseObjects.{zeroPt,onePt,inftyPt}` to build *k̄-points* of ℙ¹.
95	    It does *not* extend to the divisor-theoretic "degree-1 line bundle
96	    on C induces a map C → ℙ¹" — that requires divisor → line-bundle, line
97	    bundle → global sections, RR on H⁰, all absent.
98	  - **Hurwitz / Castelnuovo**: ABSENT.
99	  - **Function field of an irreducible scheme**: PRESENT —
100	    `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.functionField` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.FunctionField`).
101	    Useful for a function-field-theoretic route but not by itself
102	    enough.
103	  - **Rational maps**: PRESENT — `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap`
104	    (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap`). Domain
105	    of definition, extension along reduced/separated targets, etc.
106	    Adjacent to Hartshorne II.6.10.1's "rational ⟹ ≅ ℙ¹" but doesn't
107	    package the conclusion.
108	- **Project's path**: The project has NOT built a divisor stack — it
109	  deliberately demoted Route A (the FGA/Pic representability engine) off
110	  the genus-0 critical path (see iter-163 memory
111	  `[[route-c-cube-not-needed-iter163]]`). The project's only line-bundle-shaped
112	  artifact is the older `.archon/lanes/.../Picard/LineBundle.lean`
113	  scaffolds (project-internal experimental code, never landed and now
114	  abandoned per `M3` audit).
115	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost — the gap is *deliberate*. The project
116	  committed to Route C precisely *because* Route A (Pic/divisor/RR)
117	  was an unbounded sub-build. The cost of NOT having this stack is
118	  exactly the present situation: `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` has no
119	  honest in-project closure.
120	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (the gap is the *committed
121	  trade-off*; closure requires either a sub-build or a sidestep).
122	
123	### Decision 3: Per-Hartshorne-piece audit (Q3)
124	
125	For each piece of Hartshorne's IV.1.3.5 proof:
126	
127	| Hartshorne ingredient | Mathlib status |
128	|---|---|
129	| (i) Divisor `P − Q` of two closed points on a curve | ABSENT — no `WeilDivisor`/`CartierDivisor` on a scheme. |
130	| (ii) RR dimension formula `l(D) − l(K − D) = deg D + 1 − g` | ABSENT — no RR theorem. |
131	| (iii) Linear equivalence `D ∼ D'` of divisors | ABSENT — precondition (divisors) missing. |
132	| (iv) "X rational ⟹ X ≅ ℙ¹" (Hartshorne II.6.10.1 / I.6.12) | ABSENT — `RationalMap` exists but the classification "complete nonsingular curve birational to ℙ¹ ⟹ ≅ ℙ¹" is not packaged. Would need scheme-level normalisation + the equivalence "smooth proper curve ≅ DVR-valuation-ring patching". |
133	
134	- **Verdict**: every one of (i)–(iv) is NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL.
135	
136	### Decision 4: Cheapest Mathlib-supportable variant of "genus-0 ⟹ ≅ ℙ¹"
137	
138	What CAN Mathlib support TODAY?
139	
140	- The project's `genus C = 0` unfolds (via the `Scheme.HModule k F 1`
141	  layer at `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`) to
142	  `Module.finrank k (H¹(C, O_C)) = 0`, which together with finite-
143	  dimensionality (Serre, *queued* in the project per `Genus.tex
144	  §Genus_status`) gives `H¹(C, O_C) = 0` as a `k`-vector space.
145	- That alone is *not* enough to recover `C ≅ ℙ¹`. Each of the classical
146	  routes blocks:
147	  - **Divisor-theoretic (Hartshorne IV.1.3.5)**: blocked by all four
148	    pieces above.
149	  - **Direct `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` construction**: would require
150	    producing two compatible degree-1 sections of some line bundle on
151	    C. The project has `pointOfVec`-style maps from a unit-vector data
152	    into `Proj` of `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`, but those *take ℙ¹ as
153	    target*; they do not produce maps *from* C into ℙ¹.
154	  - **Function-field / valuation-theoretic** (smooth proper curve
155	    determined by `k(C)`; for genus 0 the function field is rational
156	    `k̄(t)` ⟹ ≅ ℙ¹): requires the scheme/normalization equivalence
157	    "smooth proper curve over k̄ ↔ finitely-generated field extension
158	    of trdeg 1" — present in Hartshorne I.6 but ABSENT in Mathlib.
159	  - **Pic⁰ group scheme**: this is Route A, deliberately deferred.
160	  - **Direct via Albanese**: circular (this lemma is what feeds the
161	    genus-0 Albanese witness).
162	
163	- **Conclusion**: there is no Mathlib-supported route TODAY. The
164	  cheapest variant of `C ≅ ℙ¹` Mathlib supports is *trivial*: it
165	  supports `ProjectiveLineBar kbar ≅ ProjectiveLineBar kbar` (the
166	  identity). It does not bridge `genus C = 0` to that.
167	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL.
168	
169	### Decision 5: Lean signature shape (Q5)
170	
171	- **Current**: `theorem genusZero_curve_iso_P1 … (_hgenus : genus C = 0)
172	  : Nonempty (C ≅ ProjectiveLineBar kbar)`
173	- **Consumer use** (`AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:1174`):
174	  `obtain ⟨φ⟩ := genusZero_curve_iso_P1 _hgenus; set g := φ.inv ≫ f; …`.
175	  The consumer destructs `Nonempty`, extracts `φ`, and transports a
176	  morphism along `φ`. The choice of `φ` is irrelevant downstream
177	  (`morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const g` exhibits an `a₀` for *any*
178	  such `g`).
179	- **Alternative shapes considered**:
180	  - `(p : 𝟙_ ⟶ C) → (C ≅ ProjectiveLineBar kbar)` carrying the
181	    point: the consumer ALREADY has a `k̄`-point `p` of `C`, and a
182	    pointed iso (`φ.hom ∘ p = ℙ¹.zeroPt`) would shorten the basepoint-
183	    pinning step. But the consumer's basepoint-pinning is currently
184	    one short `calc` step (`toUnit_unique` + `Category.id_comp`), so
185	    the simplification is marginal.
186	  - `(C ≅ ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊕ (genus C > 0)` (genus-stratified):
187	    redundant — `_hgenus : genus C = 0` already discriminates.
188	  - Direct conclusion (skip the iso): `(f : C ⟶ A) → … → f = toUnit C
189	    ≫ η[A]` (i.e. fold the whole `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme`
190	    body into a single lemma). This would *avoid* `genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
191	    as a standalone — but it does NOT avoid its mathematical content;
192	    the proof would still need to reduce to the ℙ¹ case somewhere.
193	- **Mathlib idiom**: `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)` is the standard "X is
194	  isomorphic to Y" shape in `CategoryTheory` (cf.
195	  `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.IsoClass`, `Nonempty (X ⟶ Y)` for
196	  "morphism exists" patterns). The current shape *is* idiomatic.
197	- **Verdict**: PROCEED. The signature is correctly shaped.
198	
199	## Recommendation
200	
201	`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` is **NOT dispatchable as a Lane B prover lane this
202	iter**. Every one of the four Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 ingredients — divisor of a
203	closed point, RR dimension formula, linear equivalence, "rational ⟹ ≅ ℙ¹" —
204	is absent from Mathlib at scheme level, with no isolated piece reachable
205	without the prerequisites of the others. There is no Mathlib-supported
206	shortcut around the RR-style sub-build.
207	
208	The planner has four real options:
209	
210	1. **(Preferred) Schedule a blueprint-writer expansion of
211	   `prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1` into a divisor-theoretic sub-build**
212	   under a new chapter section (e.g. `\section{Riemann–Roch on a
213	   curve (genus-0 case)}`). This makes the gap concrete and
214	   sub-divisible. Estimated 30–60 iters of prover work on top of the
215	   blueprint expansion. *Cost*: large; *benefit*: keeps the iso
216	   axiom-clean and re-usable, and the divisor stack feeds Route A
217	   (positive-genus Albanese) if it is ever revisited.
218	
219	2. **Defer to upstream Mathlib**: track Mathlib's nascent Pic / divisor
220	   activity (the present `CommRing.Pic` / `MeromorphicOn.divisor` /
221	   `Scheme.RationalMap` are visible threads). Pin
222	   `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` as an explicit named gap in the blueprint
223	   (already done in `rmk:genusZero_iso_subbuild`). Continue to ship
224	   the rest of the chain as axiom-clean *modulo* this single named
225	   gap, and budget zero iters on it. *Cost*: leaves the headline
226	   `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` carrying `sorryAx`; *benefit*:
227	   no project-internal time spent.
228	
229	3. **Sidestep via a different proof structure that does not reduce to
230	   ℙ¹**. Mathlib's available primitives — `rigidity_lemma`,
231	   `rigidity_eqOn_dense_open`, `ext_of_eqOnOpen`,
232	   `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` — only let us *consume* a ℙ¹-iso, not
233	   produce one. The 𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut needs ℙ¹'s scaling
234	   automorphism, which a generic genus-0 curve does NOT carry until
235	   it is identified with ℙ¹. A `df = 0` route exists in char 0 but is
236	   the project's *demoted fallback* (route (a)) and breaks in char p,
237	   contradicting the char-free commitment in `thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV`.
238	   No char-free sidestep is known. *Cost*: research-bounded;
239	   *benefit*: speculative.
240	
241	4. **User-authorised `axiom` declaration** for `genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
242	   with the matching Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 citation. *Cost*: violates
243	   the project's axiom-clean target; *benefit*: unblocks `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme`
244	   and the genus-0 Albanese witness.
245	
246	**My recommendation**: option (2) — defer to upstream Mathlib AND
247	formalize the gap as the project's *named* RR gap in the chapter (the
248	blueprint already does this at `rmk:genusZero_iso_subbuild`; no extra
249	work). Concretely, the planner should NOT open a Lane B prover lane on
250	this lemma this iter; should NOT escalate it to "dispatchable"; should
251	keep it on the "named gap" list alongside the M3 Albanese gap (per
252	`analogies/m3-route-audit.md`). If user explicitly authorises an axiom
253	(option 4), do so with the Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 citation; otherwise the
254	genus-0 headline `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` will carry a
255	propagated `sorryAx` through this lemma until upstream lands a divisor
256	stack. The signature shape (Decision 5) is correct as-is — do NOT
257	refactor.
258	
259	## Persistent file
260	- `analogies/rrbridge-survey.md` — this file, captured for future iters.

1	\chapter{Rigidity over a base field $k$: morphisms from a genus-$0$ curve to a group scheme are constant}
2	\label{chap:RigidityKbar}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/RigidityKbar.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-157): The project's COMMITTED genus-0 rigidity route is now route
6	% (c) --- the characteristic-free abelian-variety rigidity-lemma stack developed in its own
7	% chapter \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity} (Lean file
8	% AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean, upstream of Jacobian.lean). The headline
9	% there, thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV (char-free, [IsAlgClosed k-bar] only), is what
10	% genusZeroWitness consumes. THIS chapter now documents the over-k-bar declaration
11	% rigidity_over_kbar (which carries [CharZero k-bar]) together with the FALLBACK route (a):
12	% the df=0 / cotangent-triviality + Serre-duality decomposition and the closed, axiom-clean
13	% chart-algebra envelope (which supplies only the converse "df=0 implies constant"). Route
14	% (a) is retained in tree, off the critical path; the df=0 framing below is the fallback,
15	% NOT the committed route.
16	
17	This chapter introduces the project's named declaration for the M2.a sub-step of the genus-$0$ Albanese-witness argument: rigidity of morphisms from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve of genus $0$ to a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme, \textbf{over an algebraically closed base field $\bar k$ of characteristic zero} (the alg-closed pivot of \S "Iter-152 alg-closed pivot" below; descent of the constancy conclusion to a general base field $k$ is a separate downstream step, hosted in the genus-$0$ witness chapter). The classical statement (Mumford, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Chapter~II~\S4) is
18	
19	\begin{quote}
20	  \emph{Let $A$ be an abelian variety of positive dimension over an algebraically closed field $\bar k$. Then every morphism $f \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ is constant.}
21	\end{quote}
22	
23	This is the keystone classical input for the C.2.d sub-step of Jacobian.tex \S C.2: the genus-$0$ universal-property step of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} that concludes the universal-pointed-morphism factorisation by ruling out positive-dimensional images of $f \colon C \to A$ when $C$ has genus $0$ and a $k$-rational marked point.
24	
25	\paragraph{Disposition (iter-155): \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} is a gated NAMED GAP.} The named declaration \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity\_over\_kbar} is landed with a \texttt{sorry} body and is held as a \textbf{named gap} in the same family as \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} (Jacobian.tex) and the deferred Serre-duality / Riemann--Roch stack --- honestly disclosed, not on a prover lane. The substantive content (the C.2.d sub-step below, ruling out a $1$-dimensional image of $f$) reduces to the vanishing of the cotangent pull-back $\mathrm df \colon f^{*}\Omega_{A/\bar k} \to \Omega_{C/\bar k}$, and a decisive iter-155 \texttt{mathlib-analogist} consult (\texttt{analogies/df-zero-production-iter155.md}) established that \textbf{producing $\mathrm df = 0$ is irreducibly a global-sections fact}, gated on two genuinely-absent inputs:
26	
27	\begin{itemize}
28	  \item \textbf{(gap i) Global cotangent-triviality of $A$:} $\Omega_{A/\bar k} \cong \mathcal O_A^{\oplus g}$, generated by translation-invariant differentials. The presheaf globalisation that would build this (\texttt{mulRight\_globalises\_cotangent}) was \textbf{excised from the Lean tree at iter-145}; \texttt{GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity} $+$ \texttt{shearMulRight} survive only as a \emph{design template}. Estimated $\sim 800$--$1500$ LOC. Mathlib's only ``group cotangent is trivial'' idiom is the smooth-manifold \texttt{GroupLieAlgebra} stack, which does not port to schemes.
29	  \item \textbf{(gap ii) Serre duality $H^0(C, \Omega_{C/\bar k}) = 0$} for the genus-$0$ curve. Given the global trivialisation of gap (i), $\mathrm df \in H^0\bigl(C, \mathcal{H}om(f^{*}\Omega_{A/\bar k}, \Omega_{C/\bar k})\bigr) \cong \bigl(H^0(C, \Omega_{C/\bar k})\bigr)^{\oplus g}$, so $\mathrm df = 0 \iff H^0(C, \Omega_{C/\bar k}) = 0$. Mathlib snapshot \texttt{b80f227} has no dualizing sheaf and no Riemann--Roch; the project's \texttt{Cohomology/*} stack computes $H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)$ (genus) but has \textbf{no $H^0$-vanishing lemma and no genus$\leftrightarrow\Omega$ bridge}. The genus-$0$ hypothesis $H^1(C, \mathcal O_C) = 0$ does \emph{not} yield $H^0(C, \Omega_{C/\bar k}) = 0$ without the duality. Estimated $\sim 3000$--$8000$ LOC.
30	\end{itemize}
31	
32	\noindent The chart-algebra envelope of \cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile} (\cref{lem:KaehlerDifferential_mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero} (KDM), \cref{lem:constants_integral_over_base_field}, and the delegate \cref{lem:chart_algebra_df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart}), which \emph{is} closed and axiom-clean, supplies only the \textbf{converse} half of the bridge: ``if $\mathrm df = 0$ then $f$ factors through $\Spec \bar k$ (is constant)''. It \emph{cannot} produce $\mathrm df = 0$: on each affine chart $V \subseteq C$, $\Omega_{C/\bar k}(V)$ is free of rank $1$ (nonzero), so a chart-by-chart computation never detects the vanishing --- the vanishing materialises only on global sections. Earlier framings in this chapter (the piece-(iv) ``DEFERRED, not on the C.2.d critical path'' disposition and the ``No-Serre-duality layer'') asserted that the chart-algebra route \emph{avoids} Serre duality; \textbf{that is false}, and is corrected throughout \cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile}: $H^0(C, \Omega_{C/\bar k}) = 0$ is \emph{on} the critical path for producing $\mathrm df = 0$, not avoided.
33	
34	The project does \textbf{not} open a ``produce $\mathrm df = 0$'' prover lane until one of two candidate closure routes is built; the decision between them is a leading open strategic question and the chapter commits to neither:
35	\begin{itemize}
36	  \item \textbf{Route (a):} build gaps (i) $+$ (ii) directly.
37	  \item \textbf{Route (b):} the dual-abelian-variety keystone --- ``morphisms $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ are constant because $\Pic^0(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}) = 0$''. Earlier rejected as ``needs $\Pic^0$'', but since the positive-genus arm of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} (Route A) needs the $\Pic^0$ engine \emph{regardless}, route (b) may amortise the shared machinery and be globally cheaper than a standalone Serre-duality build.
38	\end{itemize}
39	
40	\paragraph{Iter-152 alg-closed pivot.} The project committed an architectural pivot: rigidity is proved \textbf{only over an algebraically closed base field $\bar k$ of characteristic zero}, and the constancy conclusion descends to a general base field $k$ downstream. The reason is that the chart-algebra core lemma \cref{lem:KaehlerDifferential_mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero} (KDM) is mathematically \emph{false} as a bare $B$-only algebra lemma: a prover lane exhibited two counterexamples that satisfy every hypothesis yet have $\ker D \supsetneq \operatorname{range}(\mathtt{algebraMap})$ --- namely $B = k \times k$ (finite \'etale over $k$) and $B = \mathbb Q(\sqrt 2)$ over $\mathbb Q$. The missing content is precisely ``$k$ algebraically closed in $B$''. Adding the \emph{joint} hypotheses \texttt{[IsAlgClosed kbar]} $+$ \texttt{[IsDomain B]} makes it true: \texttt{[IsDomain B]} kills the $B = k \times k$ counterexample and \texttt{[IsAlgClosed kbar]} kills the $\mathbb Q(\sqrt 2)/\mathbb Q$ counterexample. (Neither hypothesis suffices alone; the iter-151 note that ``\texttt{[IsDomain B]} alone fails'' considered the hypotheses only one-at-a-time.) Over an algebraically closed base the \cref{lem:constants_integral_over_base_field} constants lemma also collapses to a one-liner via \texttt{IsAlgClosed.algebraMap\_bijective\_of\_isIntegral}, and the entire (S3.\textsc{sep}/\textsc{pi}) base-change-to-$\bar k$ chain is descoped from the M2.a critical path.
41	
42	\textbf{The Lean declaration's signature now carries \texttt{[IsAlgClosed kbar]} $+$ \texttt{[CharZero kbar]}} (matching the theorem's existing docstring, which already described $\bar k$ as algebraically closed --- the hypothesis was simply absent from the formal statement). Prior strategy iterations had instead routed through base-change to $\bar k$ followed by Galois descent of morphism equality (M2.c, $\sim 4$--$8$ iter / $300$--$500$ LOC); the iter-127 framing then claimed all three pieces of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile build over an \emph{arbitrary} base field, dropping descent entirely. The alg-closed pivot supersedes that framing for the rigidity theorem itself: rigidity is established over $\bar k$, and the descent of the constancy conclusion to a general $k$ is a short downstream consequence of \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.epi\_of\_flat\_of\_surjective} (faithfully-flat covers are epimorphisms of schemes), with the detailed descent living in the genus-$0$ witness chapter. The group-scheme cotangent-triviality pieces (i)/(iii) below remain genuinely intrinsic to the group scheme and are stated over a general base where that is sound; only the curve-side $\mathrm df = 0 \Rightarrow$ constancy chain (the KDM/constants core) requires the alg-closed base.
43	
44	\section{Statement}
45	
46	% NOTE: classical rigidity for morphisms from $\mathbb P^1$ (or genus-0
47	% pointed proper curves) to abelian / group varieties; standard
48	% references are Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Ch.~II \S4 (rational
49	% curves on abelian varieties; paywalled, not bundled) and Hartshorne,
50	% *Algebraic Geometry*, Ex.~IV.4 (not bundled in references/). The
51	% cotangent-route closure used here ties into Stacks Tag 04QM (smooth
52	% over fields ⇒ geometrically reduced; references/stacks-varieties.tex,
53	% lemma-smooth-geometrically-normal) for the chart-level differential
54	% machinery.
55	\begin{theorem}
56	  \label{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}
57	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar}
58	  \leanok
59	  % SOURCE: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch. II §4 (verbatim text not yet
60	  % retrieved — paywalled, no open copy in references/). No SOURCE QUOTE
61	  % is supplied: the source is not bundled and recalled text must not be
62	  % substituted (citation discipline).
63	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, II §4.}
64	  Let $\bar k$ be an algebraically closed field of characteristic zero (Lean: \texttt{[IsAlgClosed kbar]} $+$ \texttt{[CharZero kbar]}; in the use case, the algebraic closure of a base field $k$, with descent of the conclusion to $k$ handled downstream in the genus-$0$ witness chapter). These two hypotheses match the theorem's existing docstring and are required by the curve-side $\mathrm df = 0 \Rightarrow$ constancy chain (\cref{lem:KaehlerDifferential_mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero}, \cref{lem:constants_integral_over_base_field}); see \S "Iter-152 alg-closed pivot". Let $C : \mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over $\bar k$ with $\genus(C) = 0$ (i.e.\ $\dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C) = 0$). Let $A : \mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over $\bar k$, with identity element $\eta_A \in A(\bar k)$. Let
65	  \[
66	    f \colon C \longrightarrow A
67	  \]
68	  be a morphism of $\bar k$-schemes over $\Spec \bar k$, and let $p \in C(\bar k)$ be a $\bar k$-rational point such that $f(p) = \eta_A$. Then $f$ equals the constant morphism at $\eta_A$:
69	  \[
70	    f \;=\; \bigl(C \to \Spec \bar k \xrightarrow{\,\eta_A\,} A\bigr).
71	  \]
72	
73	  \emph{Encoding note (iter-126).} The Lean signature uses the abstract characterization $\mathtt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}\ 1\ C.\mathtt{hom} + \mathtt{IsProper}\ C.\mathtt{hom} + \mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}\ C.\mathtt{hom} + \genus(C) = 0$ instead of a literal $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ scheme. The two encodings are equivalent over $\bar k$ once the curve has a $\bar k$-rational point (Brauer--Severi triviality + Riemann--Roch on $C \cong \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$); the abstract form is used because Mathlib snapshot \texttt{b80f227} does not ship a packaged $\mathtt{ProjectiveSpace}\,n\,S$ as a $\mathtt{Scheme.Over}\,S$, and the C.2.b–C.2.d proof decomposition below works for any smooth proper geometrically integral genus-0 curve over $\bar k$ without explicit $\mathbb P^1$ API.
74	\end{theorem}
75	
76	\begin{remark}
77	  \label{rem:rigidity_over_kbar_dim_dichotomy}
78	  \uses{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}
79	  The conclusion is vacuously true when $A$ is zero-dimensional: in that case $A = \Spec \bar k$ and every morphism $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ factors through $\Spec \bar k$ trivially. The substantive content of the theorem is the positive-dimensional case, which decomposes into the steps of \cref{sec:RigidityKbar_proof_decomposition}.
80	\end{remark}
81	
82	\section{Proof decomposition}
83	\label{sec:RigidityKbar_proof_decomposition}
84	
85	The proof of \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} decomposes as follows. We reuse the sub-step labelling of Jacobian.tex \S C.2 so cross-references between the two chapters remain unambiguous. \textbf{This decomposition is the eventual closing recipe for a gated named gap} (see the iter-155 disposition in the chapter introduction): the substantive step C.2.d reduces to producing $\mathrm df = 0$, which is gated on gaps (i)$+$(ii) [route (a)] or on the dual-AV keystone [route (b)]. The steps below stay inert --- the Lean body is a \texttt{sorry} --- until a route is chosen and built; none names a present, assembled Mathlib infrastructure for the image-dimension and $\mathrm df = 0$ content.
86	
87	\begin{itemize}
88	  \item[\textbf{(C.2.b)}] \emph{Reduction to the project's rigidity lemma.} Let $c \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ denote the constant morphism at $\eta_A$. The conclusion $f = c$ follows from \cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} (i.e.\ \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_eqOnOpen}, after the iter-125 refactor) applied to $g_1 := f$, $g_2 := c$, $X := \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$, $Y := A$, once we produce a non-empty open $U \subseteq \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ on which $U.\iota \circ f.\mathrm{left} = U.\iota \circ c.\mathrm{left}$ as scheme morphisms. We will take $U := \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$. The refactored rigidity lemma's hypotheses are trivially satisfied: $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ is smooth (hence reduced) and geometrically irreducible over $\bar k$, and $A$ is proper (hence separated) over $\bar k$.
89	
90	  \item[\textbf{(C.2.c)}] \emph{Image dimension argument.} The morphism $f$ goes from a proper geometrically integral $\bar k$-scheme of dimension $1$ to a smooth proper $\bar k$-scheme of dimension $g := \dim_{\bar k} A$. The set-theoretic image $f(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}) \subseteq A$ is an irreducible closed subset of $A$ (continuous image of an irreducible space under a proper map). \emph{Gating note (iter-155): this dichotomy names no present, assembled Mathlib infrastructure} --- the ``image of a proper map is closed and irreducible'' fact and a usable notion of scheme/image dimension are not packaged for this setting in snapshot \texttt{b80f227}; C.2.c is part of the gated assembly, to be detailed once a route (a)/(b) is chosen. Dichotomy:
91	  \begin{itemize}
92	    \item If $g = 0$: $A = \Spec \bar k$ and the conclusion is vacuous (\cref{rem:rigidity_over_kbar_dim_dichotomy}).
93	    \item If $g \geq 1$ and the image is a single point: the image must be $\{\eta_A\}$ (the pointed hypothesis $f(p) = \eta_A$ pins it). Then $f$ is constant on underlying topological points, and the conclusion follows by promoting set-level equality to scheme-morphism equality (sub-step C.2.e of Jacobian.tex, applied via \cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}).
94	    \item If $g \geq 1$ and the image is one-dimensional: the image is an irreducible rational curve in $A$. This case is ruled out by the keystone classical input (C.2.d below).
95	  \end{itemize}
96	
97	  \item[\textbf{(C.2.d)}] \emph{Keystone: proper rational curves on an abelian variety are constant --- the gated content.} The remaining case --- the image of $f$ has dimension exactly $1$ --- is ruled out by Mumford's classical fact stated in the chapter introduction, and is the load-bearing reduction of the whole theorem. It reduces to the vanishing $\mathrm df = 0$ of the cotangent pull-back $f^{*}\Omega_{A/\bar k} \to \Omega_{C/\bar k}$. Per the iter-155 disposition (chapter introduction) and \texttt{analogies/df-zero-production-iter155.md}, producing $\mathrm df = 0$ is a \textbf{global-sections} fact: with the global trivialisation $f^{*}\Omega_{A/\bar k} \cong \mathcal O_C^{\oplus g}$ (gap i), $\mathrm df = 0 \iff H^0(C, \Omega_{C/\bar k}) = 0$ (gap ii, Serre duality on the genus-$0$ curve). The two standard proofs sketched in Jacobian.tex \S C.2.d furnish the two gated closure routes that the project commits to neither of yet: \textbf{route (a)} = the cotangent-bundle route, building gaps (i)$+$(ii) directly (triviality of $\Omega_{A/\bar k}$ + $H^0(C,\Omega_{C/\bar k}) = 0$); \textbf{route (b)} = the dual-abelian-variety route via $\Pic^0(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}) = 0$, which may amortise the $\Pic^0$ engine that the positive-genus arm of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} needs regardless. The chart-algebra envelope of \cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile} supplies only the converse ``$\mathrm df = 0 \Rightarrow$ constant''; it does \emph{not} produce $\mathrm df = 0$, and (contrary to earlier framing) does not avoid the Serre-duality input.
98	
99	  \item[\textbf{(C.2.e)}] \emph{Promotion of set-level equality to scheme-morphism equality.} Standard reduced-source / separated-target argument, integrated into the application of \cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} in C.2.b.
100	\end{itemize}

content:
130	\paragraph{Iter-144 chart-algebra pivot --- disposition (per \texttt{mathlib-analogist-chart-algebra-iter144} + STRATEGY.md \S "Iter-144 chart-algebra pivot" block).}
131	Effective iter-145+, the M2.body-pile commits to the \textbf{chart-algebra route}. Under this commitment:
132	\begin{itemize}
133	  \item Piece (i.b) Step 2 d\_app sub-sorry inside \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj}'s proof, the IsIso theorem \cref{lem:GrpObj_basechange_along_proj_two_inv_app_isIso} (Lean target \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.basechange\_along\_proj\_two\_inv\_app\_isIso}), and piece (i.b) Main \cref{lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises} (Lean target \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.mulRight\_globalises\_cotangent}) are \textbf{DESCOPED from critical-path}. \emph{Status correction (iter-155):} \texttt{mulRight\_globalises\_cotangent} was subsequently \textbf{EXCISED from the Lean tree at iter-145}; \texttt{GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity} $+$ \texttt{shearMulRight} survive only as a \emph{design template}, \textbf{not} a present declaration. This excised gap-(i) globalisation ($\sim 800$--$1500$ LOC) is exactly the gap (i) that the keystone $\mathrm df = 0$ (chapter introduction) is gated on; the blueprint blocks below for these declarations are therefore an off-path record of the bundled-route design, not a live in-tree obligation.
134	  \item Piece (i.c.1/i.c.2/i.c.3) (\cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_free} + \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_rank_eq_dim} assembly) is \textbf{DESCOPED}. Chart-algebra routes the freeness and rank facts through chart-level \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} per-chart, not through a global sheaf-of-modules construction.
135	  \item Piece (iii) scheme-level absolute Frobenius PHANTOM (\textasciitilde$800$--$1500$ LOC, \texttt{Scheme.absoluteFrobenius} ABSENT in Mathlib snapshot \texttt{b80f227}) is \textbf{DESCOPED}; chart-algebra uses ring-level \texttt{RingHom.iterateFrobenius\_comm} from \texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.CharP.Frobenius} per-chart, giving Frobenius iteration as a direct \texttt{RingHom}-commutation with no scheme-level lift.
136	  \item Piece (ii) is \textbf{INFLATED} to absorb the chart-algebra ($\alpha$) \texttt{Algebra.IsPushout}-from-affine-product helper and ($\beta$) per-chart translation-invariance Kähler-derivation argument upstream; see the \S "Iter-144 chart-algebra envelope for piece (ii)" paragraph below for the updated envelope (\textasciitilde$600$--$1050$ LOC total).
137	\end{itemize}
138	
139	Piece (i.a) --- \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace} (\texttt{cotangentSpaceAtIdentity}) and \cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank} (\texttt{cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_finrank\_eq}), closed iter-128 $\to$ iter-132 --- remains in-tree as standalone infrastructure. Chart-algebra does \emph{not} consume it on the rigidity critical path, but the cotangent-space-at-the-identity definition and its rank are independently useful downstream (Lie-algebra dual $\mathfrak g$ on the tangent side, etc.).
140	
141	\paragraph{Iter-144 chart-algebra envelope for piece (ii) (per \texttt{mathlib-analogist-chart-algebra-iter144}).}
142	Piece (ii) (scheme-level $\mathrm df = 0 \Rightarrow f$ factors through $\Spec k$, the C.2.d / M2.a body consumer of piece (i)'s cotangent-trivial output) is the load-bearing body under the iter-144 chart-algebra pivot. Total envelope \textasciitilde$600$--$1050$ LOC, decomposed as:
143	\begin{itemize}
144	  \item Chart-algebra ($\alpha$) \texttt{Algebra.IsPushout}-from-affine-product helper: \textasciitilde$80$--$150$ LOC. Constructs the \texttt{Algebra.IsPushout} instance on the chart-level ring extensions $\Gamma(\Spec k, U) \to \Gamma(W, \mathcal O_G)$ for affine charts $V \to W$ in the product $G \otimes G$. Built from \texttt{pullbackSpecIso} and the \texttt{\_hom\_fst} / \texttt{\_inv\_fst} simp companions in \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Pullbacks}, chained with \texttt{CommRingCat.isPushout\_iff\_isPushout} and \texttt{isPullback\_SpecMap\_of\_isPushout} (cf.\ \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj} Route (a) item 1).
145	  \item Chart-algebra ($\beta$) per-chart translation-invariance Kähler-derivation argument: \textasciitilde$150$--$300$ LOC. The load-bearing core of M2.a's $\mathrm df = 0$ derivation chain (per STRATEGY.md \S "Soundness rules: M2.a $\mathrm df = 0$ derivation chain --- articulated (added iter-144)"). The three-layer chain is:
146	    \begin{enumerate}
147	      \item \textbf{Chart-local layer.} On each affine chart $W = \Spec B \subseteq A.\mathrm{left}$ with affine $V \subseteq C.\mathrm{left}$ such that $f$ restricts to a ring map $f^{\sharp} \colon B \to \Gamma(C, V)$, the differential $\mathrm df = 0$ at the chart level means the composite $\Omega_{B/k} \to \Omega_{\Gamma(C, V)/k}$ obtained from the universal Kähler derivation $\mathrm d \colon B \to \Omega_{B/k}$ post-composed with $f^{\sharp}$ vanishes. Combined with piece (i)'s chart-level free-of-rank-$n$ presentation of $\Omega_{B/k}$ (\texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential}), the kernel-extraction step gives $f^{\sharp}(b) \in \Gamma(C, V)^{p^{e}}$ for every $b \in B$, where $p = \operatorname{char} k$ and $e \geq 0$ is a chart-uniform Frobenius depth.
148	      \item \textbf{Char-$p$ Frobenius-iteration layer.} The chart-local conclusion $f^{\sharp}(b) \in \Gamma(C, V)^{p^{e}}$ is promoted to "for every $b \in B$, there exists $e$ with $f^{\sharp}(b) = \mathrm{iterateFrobenius}_{p, e}(c_{b})$ for some $c_{b} \in \Gamma(C, V)$" via \texttt{RingHom.iterateFrobenius\_comm} (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.CharP.Frobenius}, verified iter-144). The uniform-$e$-on-$W$ glue extracts a globally-bounded Frobenius depth on $C$ from the smoothness of $A$ at the image of $f$ (using $A$'s tangent-bundle triviality at each point in the image of $f$ to control the depth uniformly across $W$). In characteristic $0$ this layer is vacuous: $\mathrm df = 0 \Rightarrow f^{\sharp}(b) \in k \subseteq \Gamma(C, V)$ directly via the standard derivation-kernel argument (no Frobenius).
149	      \item \textbf{Constancy-from-$\mathrm df = 0$ layer (the converse only).} \emph{Iter-155 correction.} This layer was previously titled the ``No-Serre-duality layer'' and claimed the chart-algebra ($\beta$) chain ``replaces'' the $H^0(C, \Omega_{C/k}) = 0$ route, ``eliminating piece (iv) as a critical-path dependency''. \textbf{That claim is false} (per \texttt{analogies/df-zero-production-iter155.md}) and is corrected here. What the chain actually delivers is the \emph{converse} implication: \textbf{given} the chart-uniform input $f^{\sharp}(b) \in \Gamma(C, V)^{p^{e}}$ (which is the chart-level shadow of the hypothesis $\mathrm df = 0$), the conclusion ``$f$ factors through $\Spec k$'' follows from the integrally-closed-constants helper (next item) and the scheme-level lift via \texttt{Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_eqOnOpen} (final item). It does \emph{not} produce the hypothesis $\mathrm df = 0$, and it does not avoid the $H^0(C, \Omega_{C/k}) = 0$ input: that input is exactly what is needed to \emph{establish} $\mathrm df = 0$ in the first place. Concretely, $\mathrm df = 0$ is a global-sections fact --- with the gap-(i) trivialisation $f^{*}\Omega_{A/k} \cong \mathcal O_C^{\oplus g}$, $\mathrm df \in \bigl(H^0(C, \Omega_{C/k})\bigr)^{\oplus g}$, so $\mathrm df = 0 \iff H^0(C, \Omega_{C/k}) = 0$ --- and the chart-by-chart machinery cannot detect it because $\Omega_{C/k}(V)$ is free of rank $1$ (nonzero) on every affine chart $V$. Hence piece (iv) (or an equivalent yielding $H^0(C, \Omega_{C/k}) = 0$) is \textbf{on} the critical path for the keystone, not eliminated by this chain.
150	    \end{enumerate}
151	  \item Algebra-level core \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.mem\_range\_algebraMap\_of\_D\_eq\_zero}: \textasciitilde$200$--$350$ LOC. Mathlib-shaped helper for the ring-level half of $\mathrm df = 0 \Rightarrow f^{\sharp}$ factors through $k$, packaged as the statement "for $D \colon R \to M$ the universal Kähler derivation with $D \circ f^{\sharp} = 0$, the image of $f^{\sharp}$ lies in the integral closure of the image of the base ring". This is the chart-uniform core consumed by layer (1) of the $\beta$-chain.
152	  \item Integrally-closed-constants helper: \textasciitilde$50$--$100$ LOC. The wrapper around "constants in $C$ are integral over $k$ for $C$ smooth proper geometrically irreducible", used to promote the chart-uniform Frobenius-depth conclusion to a global $\Spec k$-factorisation.
153	  \item Scheme-level lift via \texttt{Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_eqOnOpen}: \textasciitilde$100$--$150$ LOC. Closes the argument by exhibiting a non-empty open $U \subseteq C$ on which $f|_{U}$ equals the constant morphism at the image of $f$'s chart-uniform output, then applies the iter-125-refactored \cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} (\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_eqOnOpen}) to globalise.
154	\end{itemize}

The chart-algebra ($\beta$) chain is the \emph{converse} ``$\mathrm df = 0 \Rightarrow$ constant''; producing $\mathrm df = 0$ remains a global-sections fact gated on $H^0(C, \Omega_{C/k}) = 0$ (piece (iv)) plus the gap-(i) trivialisation. The Lean target name for the scheme-level converse half is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_diff\_zero} (per the iter-126 analogist's idiom alignment); see \cref{lem:Scheme_Over_ext_of_diff_zero} for the present thin-wrapper Lean decl versus the gated substantive $\mathrm df = \mathrm dg$ refinement.

160	Throughout this subsection, $k$ is an arbitrary field and $G : \Over\,(\Spec k)$ is a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over $k$, with identity section $\eta_G : \mathbf{1} \to G$.

163	[(i.a) Cotangent space at the identity of a group scheme]
164	  \label{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}

167	  % iter-128 hardcoded rel-dim-1 form so the same declaration serves
168	  % consumers of arbitrary relative dimension, e.g. \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}
169	  % applied to an abelian variety of dimension $g$):

171	  %       [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
172	  %       [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] : ModuleCat k
173	  The pullback along the identity section $\eta_G$ of the relative cotangent presheaf,
174	  \[
175	    \mathfrak g^{\vee} \;:=\; \eta_G^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k},
176	  \]
177	  is a finitely generated free $k$-module. We call $\mathfrak g^{\vee}$ the \emph{cotangent space of $G$ at the identity}. The \emph{Lie algebra of $G$} as a $k$-vector space is recovered as the $k$-linear dual $\mathfrak g := \mathfrak g^{\vee\vee} = \mathrm{Hom}_{k}(\mathfrak g^{\vee}, k)$ (the bracket structure is not used in the rigidity argument and is not packaged here).
178	
179	  \emph{Lean encoding note (iter-129 dualisation pinning).} The Lean declaration \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity} returns a \texttt{ModuleCat k} which is the \emph{un-dualised} cotangent $\mathfrak g^{\vee}$ — the direct output of the pullback-along-section construction described in the proof, with no internal \texttt{Module.Dual} hop. Downstream consumers that need the tangent $\mathfrak g$ take \texttt{Module.Dual k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G)} externally. This convention pins what was left open in the iter-128 encoding note: the rigidity argument is symmetric under dualisation (only the finite-rank-free claim is consumed), so the un-dualised form is preferred as it matches the body construction without an extra contravariant hop. The declaration embeds into Mathlib's \texttt{ModuleCat} infrastructure and supplies the \texttt{Module.Free k} / \texttt{Module.Finite k} typeclasses needed for the rank lemma \cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank} via the bridge lemma \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge}.

182	\begin{proof}
183	
184	\leanok
185	  The identity section $\eta_G$ is a canonical $k$-rational point of $G$ supplied by the $\GrpObj$ structure. The iter-131 Lean construction (Replacement (B), pure-term \texttt{noncomputable def}) realises $\mathfrak g^{\vee} = \eta_G^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$ as a chart-base-changed Kähler module: by the forward Jacobian criterion \cref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} (already in the tree as \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth\_locally\_free\_omega} in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean}), there exists an affine chart $V \subseteq G$ around the image of the identity section $\eta_G(\mathrm{pt})$, with affine base chart $U \subseteq \Spec k$ and the structural ring map $\Gamma(\Spec k, U) \to \Gamma(G, V)$ exhibiting \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension} $n$ (and consequently $\Omega_{\Gamma(G,V) \,/\, \Gamma(\Spec k,\,U)}$ is a free $\Gamma(G, V)$-module of rank $n$ by \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} in \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}). The cotangent space at the identity is then defined as the base change to $k$ of this algebraic Kähler module along the ring map $\psi_V \colon \Gamma(G, V) \to k$ obtained by restricting the identity section $\eta_G$ to $V$ and composing with the canonical isomorphism $\Gamma(\Spec k, \cdot) \cong k$:
186	  \[
187	    \mathfrak g^{\vee} \;\;:=\;\; k \,\otimes_{\Gamma(G,\,V)}\, \Omega_{\Gamma(G,\,V) \,/\, \Gamma(\Spec k,\,U)}.
188	  \]
189	  This is a finitely generated free $k$-module of rank $n$ by base-change of a free module along $\psi_V$ (the rank is preserved; see \cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank}).
190	
191	  \emph{Caveat on canonicity.} The construction depends on a chart choice $V$ extracted via a \texttt{Classical.choose}-chain from the existential statement of \texttt{smooth\_locally\_free\_omega}; it is therefore not canonically attached to $G$ in the strictest sense. (The iter-131 fix-up refactored what was previously a top-level \texttt{Classical.choice}-wrapper into a pure-term \texttt{let}-chain of \texttt{Classical.choose} / \texttt{Classical.choose\_spec} accessors, exposing the outer head symbol \texttt{(ModuleCat.extendScalars\,$\psi_V$.hom).obj\,(ModuleCat.of\,$\Gamma$(G, V)\,$\Omega$[$\Gamma$(G, V)\,/\,$\Gamma$(Spec k, U)])} to downstream consumers; see \S "Iter-131 \texttt{Classical.choose}-chain body shape" below for the Lean encoding details, and the companion \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace_extendScalars_witness} for the structural-shape rewrite handle.) For the live consumer (rigidity over $k$, via \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}), the existence of a rank-$n$ free $k$-module is the only structural content needed, so canonicity is not load-bearing. A canonical (chart-independent) presentation via the stalk-side cotangent $\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}/\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2}$ is available as a future bridge (\cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge} below, currently \texttt{\textbackslash notready}) at additional cost; the project defers that bridge until a non-rigidity consumer materialises that requires canonicity.
192	\end{proof}

271

273	  \[

331	  \[

333	    \texttt{Module.finrank}\;R\;(\texttt{TensorProduct}\;S\;R\;M')

369	  % for the rigidity application):
370	  %   def shearMulRight {C : Type*} [Category C] [CartesianMonoidalCategory C]
371	  %       (G : C) [GrpObj G] : G ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ G where
372	  %     hom := lift (fst G G) μ

378	  Let $G$ be a group object in a Cartesian monoidal category. The \emph{binary-product shear isomorphism} is the self-iso
379	  \[
380	    \sigma \colon G \otimes G \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\; G \otimes G
381	  \]
382	  whose forward and inverse maps are built categorically from the multiplication $\mu$, the inverse $\iota$, and the binary-product projections of $G \otimes G$:
383	  \[
384	    \sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \;=\; \texttt{lift\,(fst\,G\,G)\,μ}, \qquad
385	    \sigma_{\mathrm{inv}} \;=\; \texttt{lift\,(fst\,G\,G)\,(lift\,(fst\,G\,G\,≫\,$\iota$)\,(snd\,G\,G)\,≫\,μ)},
386	  \]
387	  informally $\sigma\colon (a, b) \mapsto (a, a \cdot b)$ with inverse $(a, b) \mapsto (a, a^{-1} \cdot b)$. The forward map is an isomorphism over the first projection and acts as right-translation on the second coordinate; concretely,
388	  \[
389	    \sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \pr_{1} \;=\; \pr_{1}, \qquad
390	    \sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \pr_{2} \;=\; \mu.
391	  \]
392	\end{lemma}

395	  \uses{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}
396	  \leanok
397	  The compositions $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \sigma_{\mathrm{inv}} = \mathrm{id}_{G \otimes G} = \sigma_{\mathrm{inv}} \circ \sigma_{\mathrm{hom}}$ are verified componentwise via the binary-product universal property (\texttt{CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom\_ext}). The first-projection components are trivial (both sides collapse to $\pr_{1}$ by \texttt{lift\_fst}). The second-projection components reduce to identities of the form
398	  \[
399	    \texttt{lift\,(fst\,G\,G\,≫\,$\iota$)\,$\mu$\,≫\,$\mu$} \;=\; \pr_{2}
400	    \quad\text{and}\quad
401	    \texttt{lift\,(fst\,G\,G)\,(lift\,(fst\,G\,G\,≫\,$\iota$)\,(snd\,G\,G)\,≫\,$\mu$)\,≫\,$\mu$} \;=\; \pr_{2},
402	  \]
403	  closed using the Mathlib calculus \texttt{MonObj.lift\_lift\_assoc} (associativity-via-lift reshuffling), \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.lift\_comp\_inv\_left} / \texttt{lift\_comp\_inv\_right} (left/right-inverse axioms for $\GrpObj$), and \texttt{MonObj.lift\_comp\_one\_left} (left-unit axiom). The companion identifications $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \pr_{1} = \pr_{1}$ and $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \pr_{2} = \mu$ follow directly from \texttt{lift\_fst} and \texttt{lift\_snd} on $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} = \texttt{lift\,(fst\,G\,G)\,μ}$ and are auto-generated by the \texttt{@[simps]} attribute on \texttt{shearMulRight} into the lemmas \texttt{shearMulRight\_hom\_fst} and \texttt{shearMulRight\_hom\_snd}. The proof shape parallels \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight} (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp\_.lean:277--281}) and \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.isPullback} (\texttt{Grp\_.lean:293--323}), but with both factors of $G \otimes G$ free rather than the one-input version's fixed global element.
